_comment: Curated, hand-written, source-backed Nationals facts for the Nats Nation page (On This Day + Player Spotlight). These are CURATED content — labeled curated:true / ai_generated:false in happenings.json. Every entry must be factually accurate and evergreen (no live stats that go out of date). 'On This Day' entries fire only on their matching MM-DD. Player spotlights rotate daily. Add freely; keep it true.

entries.text: On this day in 2008, Nationals Park opened — and Ryan Zimmerman walked it off with a home run against the Braves in the very first game.
On this day in 2016, Max Scherzer struck out 20 Detroit Tigers, tying the modern record for strikeouts in a nine-inning game.
On this day in 2019, the Nationals won Game 7 in Houston to capture the franchise's first World Series title.

player_spotlights.name: CJ Abrams
player_spotlights.text: The Nationals' dynamic young shortstop, acquired in the 2022 Juan Soto trade and now a cornerstone of the rebuild.

player_spotlights.name: James Wood
player_spotlights.text: A towering, high-ceiling outfielder who also came to Washington in the Juan Soto trade.

player_spotlights.name: MacKenzie Gore
player_spotlights.text: The hard-throwing left-handed starter and another key piece of the return in the Juan Soto trade.

player_spotlights.name: Dylan Crews
player_spotlights.text: The No. 2 overall pick in the 2023 MLB Draft out of LSU, headlining the next wave of Nationals talent.
